methodology.score: 50% quarterly Fundamental Health + 50% quarterly Momentum minus confirmed deterioration modifiers
methodology.displayed_growth: reported latest-quarter year-on-year growth; normalization is used only inside ranking signals
methodology.contraction_guard: acceleration is capped at 50 when both quarterly top-line and NPAT-MI YoY are negative and at 25 when both are at or below -15%; both negative also triggers a 7-point Contraction modifier
methodology.annualization: quarterly ROE and ROA use quarterly NPAT multiplied by four over average balance-sheet values
methodology.roa_thresholds: score-100 anchors are CT 10%, NH 2%, CK 8%, and BH 3% annualized quarterly ROA
methodology.margin_health: 70% company own-history score plus 30% frozen sector-level score
methodology.corporate_opm: quarterly core operating margin equals gross profit plus reported selling and G&A expense lines divided by revenue; financial income, financial expenses, and associate/JV profit are excluded
methodology.cash_quality: rolling 12-month CFO/NPAT and accrual quality; Cash-gap requires weak rolling 12-month and recent two-quarter conversion; detail values now show the same rolling inputs used by the score
methodology.corporate_interest_burden: quarterly net interest expense equals max(P&L interest expense minus note interest income, zero) and is divided by core EBIT (gross profit less selling and G&A expenses); corporate resilience weights are 55% Net D/E, 25% current ratio, and 20% net interest burden; score-100/score-0 burden anchors are 15%/50%
methodology.interest_burden_warning: Int. burden is triggered above 25% Net interest / core EBIT or when core EBIT is non-positive with positive net interest expense; it adds no separate score penalty
methodology.bank_quality: 30% NPL ratio, 20% Group-2/special-mentioned loan ratio, 25% loan-loss coverage, and 25% CIR; annualized quarterly credit cost is displayed but not scored to avoid double counting provisions already reflected in earnings
methodology.broker_resilience: 45% funding gap/equity, 30% margin loans/equity, and 25% FVTPL assets/equity; resilience is capped when margin loans, total debt, short-term funding, or fixed assets reach 90% of an observable regulatory ceiling
methodology.broker_investment_yield: annualized recurring financial-asset income divided by average loans, FVTPL, AFS, and HTM assets; displayed as a diagnostic and excluded from scoring because margin income and investment returns already affect Core revenue, OPM, ROA, and ROE
methodology.insurance_models: life-dominant insurers are detected from persistent mathematical-reserve movements and use profit/premium rather than underwriting margin; non-life insurers retain underwriting margin and investment-contribution quality tests
methodology.annual_structural: latest TTM plus five completed fiscal years; 60% structural health and 40% structural trend, with annual changes normalized against the rolling five-year median and three-year CAGRs; full local history is used before compact export
methodology.sector_details: consolidated member numerators and denominators; bank NIM is loan-weighted where an earning-asset denominator is unavailable
